Latest Insights

Dive deeper into our findings

  • Slaughtering Industry (AI-generated content for testing)

    Impacts: social and environmental

    The slaughtering industry is a critical yet often opaque component of global food systems. It represents the transition point between livestock production and meat consumption, linking agriculture, processing, distribution, and retail.

    Read More

  • Pet Trade (AI-generated content for testing)

    Tracing Pet Trade Origins

    The global pet trade is a vast and multifaceted system that connects wildlife, domesticated species, breeding industries, and international markets.

    Read More

  • Palm Oil in Cosmetics (AI-generated content for testing)

    Industries: agriculture and food production

    Impacts: deforestation, biodiversity-loss, and environmental

    Palm oil is one of the most widely used ingredients in modern cosmetics, valued for its versatility, stability, and cost efficiency. Yet behind its functional role lies a complex global supply chain tied to agriculture, processing, and environmental impact.

    Read More

  • Fishing (AI-Generated Content for testing)

    Industries: agriculture, food, mining, and food production

    Impacts: environmental

    Fishing is one of humanity’s oldest practices, deeply embedded in survival, culture, and global trade. What begins as a simple act of catching fish unfolds into a complex system of ecosystems, technologies, and supply chains.

    Read More

  • Tracing Coffee Origins (AI-generated content for testing)

    Industries: food and mining

    Impacts: social and environmental

    Coffee is more than a daily ritual—it is a global narrative woven from geography, culture, ecology, and trade. Every cup carries a story that begins long before beans are roasted and brewed.

    Read More
